Dashed lines indicate across-system means. Pearson correlation between the two metrics is reported.Right:Per-question(Dq,Aq)(D_{q},A_{q})distributions for the two circled systems; bubble size encodes item density, and stars mark across-question means.

Figure 4:Effect of one-round social exposure on system-level diversity and alignment.Each value is the change relative to the static system of Section5. All systems lose diversity (Δ​D<0\Delta D<0), while alignment generally rises but by a much smaller margin.
Figure 5:System diversity over five rounds of interactionfor six representative systems.

Figure 6:Collective decision-making outcomes in the Participatory Budgeting task.Both systems useclaude-opus-4.7as the backbone model.
The results show that the high-diversity system distributes support across substantially broader societal dimensions.
